tasks.label: Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished

memory.summary: With only ten percent left and no other deadlines looming, I can focus entirely on finishing this without rushing. It's good to know the rest of my day is free for a proper meal with the cohort.
Relief washes over me now that the deadline has passed; I can finally stop worrying about this task and focus on connecting with my friends.

desires: Ensure the professor sees me as a reliable teammate who delivered on time, preserving my social standing in the cohort.
Avoid the anxiety of having unfinished work hanging over my head so I can fully relax and enjoy my meal with friends tonight.
Smoothly transition out of 'work mode' without feeling like I'm abandoning the task halfway through.

goals.description: Join cohort_3 for dinner immediately to decompress and maintain our high-trust bond, ensuring I leave work mode behind.
goals.rationale: With the assignment submitted, my primary affiliation drive is now satisfied. My avoidant nature craves friction-free relaxation with trusted friends rather than lingering on tasks. This meal is the necessary emotional payoff that allows me to recharge for the long-term horizon.

goals.description: Leave filler_task completely untouched until Friday to preserve energy for social connection tonight.
goals.rationale: This task offers no immediate relational or academic payoff. Engaging with it now would create unnecessary cognitive load and potential conflict with my desire to be fully present with friends. Deferring it aligns with my deliberate planning and risk-averse approach to stress management.

agent.last_reasoning: Assignment is submitted; no friction remains. My priority shifts to joining cohort_3 for dinner to decompress and preserve our bond. Working now wastes energy needed for social connection and creates unnecessary stress before relaxation.

goals.description: Finish the final 10% of the assignment and submit before 13:30 to secure my standing with prof_assignment.

goals.description: Eat a meal with cohort_3 without discussing the stress of the assignment, preserving our closeness.
goals.rationale: Relationships are my highest priority (0.5). Showing up exhausted or resentful would damage the high trust (7) and closeness (7) I have with them. My deliberate nature suggests planning this now to ensure I'm present for the evening payoff.

goals.description: Leave filler_task untouched until Friday; it offers no future payoff that outweighs my current social needs.
goals.rationale: My risk tolerance is low (3) and procrastination bias is moderate (2), but since this task has zero immediate impact on my relationships or grades, avoiding friction by ignoring it aligns with my avoidant conflict style and long-term focus on meaningful interactions.

desires: To finish this assignment just enough to avoid letting the professor down, but without spending so much energy that I ruin my evening with the cohort.
To get this submission out of the way so I can fully relax and not feel like a failure if I don't do anything else today.
To maintain my trustworthiness in the eyes of the group by showing up to our meal present and happy, rather than exhausted or resentful.
